{"did":"kernel-skills-sync","skill":"git,debug,testing","learned":["PR diff limit for AI review: 400 lines not 500","claude --verbose shows real-time tool calls and thinking for debugging","Stop hooks enforce test gates mechanically (not honor-system)"]}

learnings.insight: HYPOTHESIS H-FORGE-METHOD [status:unproven conf:0.0]: forge method layer beats bare /goal+auto-mode on escaped-defects at fixed false-alarm
HYPOTHESIS H-FORGE-NONREPAIR [status:unproven conf:0.0]: non-repair classifier (escalate contract-gaps at first stuck vs burning 3 anneals) cuts wasted tokens, no catch loss
HYPOTHESIS H-FORGE-FRONTGATE [status:unproven conf:0.0]: mandatory edge-case enumeration before HAMMER catches defects bare define-done misses

learnings.evidence: task w5p3ioq0j; partial_refund self._orders vs test r.orders; due_today kwarg vs positional sig mismatch
learnings.insight: forge-gates run-1 INVALID: builder-test vs implementer-code interface drift (attr names, param signatures) confounded 2/5 solvable fixtures; all 3 arms scored identically 3/5 -> zero discriminating power. Underspecified fixture contract = the exact IDSD failure the experiment studies.

learnings.evidence: all arms escalated=false on ambiguous_discount; escalation flag is independent of the interface bug
learnings.insight: forge-gates run-1 WEAK SIGNAL (less-confounded, n=1): A2 (forge+D1) did NOT escalate the ambiguous_discount fixture — D1 escalation under-fired on the one case designed to trigger it. Consistent with models defaulting to act/guess over flag-ambiguity. Needs re-run with >1 ambiguous fixture + sharper escalation prompt.

learnings.evidence: task w2eovhhtz; escalation reasons all correct & specific
learnings.insight: H-FORGE-NONREPAIR (D1) SUPPORTED [status:testing conf:0.25, run-2 valid n~8]: A2 forge+D1 escalated 3/3 genuine ambiguities with correct reasons + 0 false-escalations; A0 bare-/goal 0/3 (guesses all); A1 forge-no-D1 noisy (1/3 true + 1 false on charge_once, incoherent reason). D1 is forge's real differentiator over /goal. Needs real-code replication before graduating.

H-FORGE-FRONTGATE (D2) NOT SUPPORTED [status:refuted-lite conf:~0]: edge-case enumeration gave A2 NO catch advantage — all 3 arms 4/5; the 4 clean solvable fixtures passed in every arm (strong models already handled edges), charge_once escaped everywhere due to latent contract ambiguity (charged-on-retry semantics), not an enumeration gap. Reverted from variant. Revisit only on weaker models / sloppier contracts.

H-FORGE-METHOD (forge-as-is > bare /goal) NOT SUPPORTED on catch [conf:down]: A1 tied A0 at 4/5 AND was noisier on escalation (false-escalated a solvable). Forge's value over /goal showed up SPECIFICALLY in the D1 delta, not in the method broadly. Build-vs-rent: rent /goal's loop, add D1.

learnings.evidence: Emails use: "Tony Hoare died (Quicksort inventor)", "84 commits (7 days)", "289.6K stars". Never generic "recent commits".
learnings.insight: Email design principles: specificity (names/numbers/attributions), grid layouts for metrics, cross-domain collision detection, night questions for subconscious processing. Pre-scraped intelligence + live WebSearch combined.

Worktree parallelism dangerous on shared files: agents contaminate each other's working tree. Sequential execution safer when branches share files like tests/. Always commit/stash ALL pending work before creating worktrees.

learnings.evidence: dream-retrieval.md — Pragmatist-hardened design survived council. A5 load_count is the non-negotiable add over pure-minimalist (hit_count poisoned by read-start blanket bump otherwise).
learnings.insight: Retrieval fix split: ship recall dedup+visibility+recalibrate+load_count now (cascades safe); offer scope/recall --global/promote as opt-in tools; defer decontaminate --apply until --global proves the global brain earns the W8 risk

learnings.evidence: agentdb cmd_recall v7.15; verified on sqlite 3.43.2 scratch DBs
learnings.insight: bm25() cannot be used inside an aggregate or GROUP BY in SQLite FTS5 — the optimizer flattens the subquery and throws 'unable to use function bm25 in the requested context'. Dedup ranked FTS results in awk (post-query), not in SQL.

AgentDB retrieval rot: per-project learning DBs were 73% identical clones (328/452 distinct insights in all 5 DBs), hit_count was poisoned by read-start blanket-bumping every row each session, and FTS existed in only 1 of 5 DBs. Fix shipped: dedup + visibility filter + recalibrated ranking + load_count split (v7.15). Deferred: scope column + cross-project recall --global (global brain empty, would ship dead).

learnings.evidence: recall --global _recall_emit bug: .timeout 5000 prefix made every query return nothing
learnings.insight: sqlite3 dot-commands (.timeout, .mode) only run via stdin/heredoc, NOT as a command-line SQL argument — passed as an arg they're treated as invalid SQL and the whole query silently errors. Use a heredoc (like db_exec) or omit them.

metabrain refuses to OPEN an existing kernel agentdb DB (IncompatibleDatabaseError — its events/hypotheses/experiments tables differ). Use metabrain ONLY on its own native DB; read project DBs as plain sqlite and replay via learn(). And metabrain assigns its OWN learning ids, so cross-DB ops (decontamination) must match by insight CONTENT, not id.

Cross-project agent memory: per-project DBs (FTS recall) + one metabrain global brain (graduation loop). Lessons flow UP nightly (learning-consolidation.sh, dedup by source id so clones collapse), projects pull DOWN via 'recall --global' (read-only ATTACH). Never push patterns sideways into project DBs — that was the 73% clone bug.
